content: Workforce strengthening strategies: (1) Expand training capacity - increase medical/nursing school seats, accelerate programs (3-year MD programs); (2) Rural training tracks - required rural rotations, rural campuses showing 2-3x higher rural practice rates; (3) Financial incentives - loan repayment ($50,000-100,000 for 3-5 years rural service), rural salary supplements; (4) Task shifting - training community health workers, nurses, pharmacists for roles traditionally physician-only (HIV ART initiation, chronic disease management) with equivalent outcomes; (5) Team-based care - optimizing scope of practice, reducing administrative burden; (6) International recruitment ethics - bilateral agreements compensating source countries. Ethiopia's Health Extension Program trained 40,000 health workers achieving 85% immunization coverage.
